title: Enhancing Analogical Reasoning through Case-Based Approaches: Integrating Conceptual Analogy and Distributed Processing
abstract: Analogical reasoning, a fundamental cognitive process, has been increasingly explored within the domain of artificial intelligence, particularly through case-based reasoning (CBR) methodologies. This paper delves into the enhancement of analogical reasoning by integrating conceptual analogy and distributed processing techniques. Drawing from foundational CBR principles, we explore how conceptual analogy can automate the extraction of essential knowledge for complex design tasks, while distributed processing models can optimize case retrieval in large-scale systems. We present a hybrid framework that leverages the strengths of both approaches, demonstrating its applicability through a series of experiments in diverse domains. Our results indicate significant improvements in reasoning accuracy and efficiency, suggesting promising avenues for future research in intelligent systems.
